the_signal.headline: Process orchestration becomes the invisible GTM infrastructure layer
the_signal.body: n8n's expanded content on orchestration tools and complex agent patterns signals that revenue teams now need orchestration as fundamental stack—not just workflow automation. This matters because GTM engineers are debugging multi-agent failures without proper architecture; orchestration platforms are becoming the difference between deployable vs. firefighting revenue systems.

tool_drop.name: WorkClaw
tool_drop.what: Workflow automation tool surfaced on Product Hunt today.
tool_drop.why: GTM teams are clearly hunting for better workflow tooling—track this category as potential alternative to n8n/Zapier for revenue automation.

thread_of_day.title: LLM Routing: From Strategy Selection to Production Architecture
thread_of_day.takeaway: Per-request dynamic model selection is now table-stakes—teams using static model routing are leaving accuracy, latency, and cost optimization on the table in production revenue systems.

quote.text: Your first AI agent worked great. Then you added three more and the system became impossible to debug.
quote.source: n8n Blog — Production AI Playbook: Complex Agent Patterns

whats_moving.context: n8n publishing deep dives on orchestration patterns and architecture suggests this moved from 'nice-to-have' to 'required infrastructure' for production multi-agent systems.
Shift from static to per-request model selection is now being formalized in production playbooks—cost and latency optimization through intelligent routing is becoming standard GTM practice.
n8n's comparative content on integration patterns indicates the market is consolidating around specific architectural approaches—standardization reduces decision friction for GTM engineers.

act_on_this: Audit your current multi-agent revenue system for observability gaps using the LLM observability checklist (metrics, failure modes, feedback loops)—chances are you're debugging blind if you built before June 2026.

numbers.stat: n8n targeting 200-person UK team by 2029; UK headcount already tripled in past year
numbers.context: The orchestration/AI agent market is consolidating capital and talent around proven platforms—this signals which vendors are winning production deployments at scale.
